2603.11679.title: LLMs can construct powerful representations and streamline sample-efficient supervised learning
2603.11679.paper_summary: As real-world datasets become increasingly complex and heterogeneous, supervised learning is often bottlenecked by input representation design. Modeling multimodal data for downstream tasks, such as time-series, free text, and structured records, often requires non-trivial domain-specific engineering. We propose an agentic pipeline to streamline this process. First, an LLM analyzes a small but diverse subset of text-serialized input examples in-context to synthesize a global rubric, which acts as a programmatic specification for extracting and organizing evidence. This rubric is then used to transform naive text-serializations of inputs into a more standardized format for downstream models. We also describe local rubrics, which are task-conditioned summaries generated by an LLM. Across 15 clinical tasks from the EHRSHOT benchmark, our rubric-based approaches significantly outperform traditional count-feature models, naive text-serialization-based LLM baselines, and a clinical foundation model, which is pretrained on orders of magnitude more data. Beyond performance, rubrics offer several advantages for operational healthcare settings such as being easy to audit, cost-effectiveness to deploy at scale, and they can be converted to tabular representations that unlock a swath of machine learning techniques.
2603.11679.paper_summary_zh: 隨著現實世界數據集變得越來越複雜和異質，監督學習常常受到輸入表示設計的瓶頸。對於下游任務建模多模態數據，例如時間序列、自由文本和結構化記錄，通常需要非平凡的特定領域工程。我們提出了一個代理管道來簡化這個過程。首先，一個大型語言模型（LLM）在上下文中分析一小部分但多樣的文本序列化輸入示例，以合成一個全球標準，該標準作為提取和組織證據的程序性規範。然後，這個標準被用來將天真的文本序列化輸入轉換為更標準化的格式，以便於下游模型使用。我們還描述了局部標準，這是由LLM生成的任務條件摘要。在EHRSHOT基準的15個臨床任務中，我們基於標準的方法顯著超越了傳統的計數特徵模型、基於天真的文本序列化的LLM基準以及一個在數量級上預訓練了更多數據的臨床基礎模型。除了性能之外，標準還為運營醫療保健環境提供了幾個優勢，例如易於審計、在規模上部署的成本效益，並且可以轉換為表格表示，從而解鎖一系列機器學習技術。
2603.11679.author: Ilker Demirel et.al.
2603.11679.authors: Ilker Demirel, Larry Shi, Zeshan Hussain, David Sontag
